NKJV.1: Now I say that the heir, as long as he is a child, does not differ at all from a slave, though he is master of all,
NKJV.2: but is under guardians and stewards until the time appointed by the father.
NKJV.3: Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world.
NKJV.4: But when the fullness of the time had come, God sent forth His Son, born of a woman, born under the law,
NKJV.5: to redeem those who were under the law, that we might receive the adoption as sons.
NKJV.6: And because you are sons, God has sent forth the Spirit of His Son into your hearts, crying out, “Abba, Father!”
NKJV.7: Therefore you are no longer a slave but a son, and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
NKJV.8: But then, indeed, when you did not know God, you served those which by nature are not gods.
NKJV.9: But now after you have known God, or rather are known by God, how is it that you turn again to the weak and beggarly elements, to which you desire again to be in bondage?
NKJV.10: You observe days and months and seasons and years.
NKJV.11: I am afraid for you, lest I have labored for you in vain.
NKJV.12: Brethren, I urge you to become like me, for I became like you. You have not injured me at all.
NKJV.13: You know that because of physical infirmity I preached the gospel to you at the first.
NKJV.14: And my trial which was in my flesh you did not despise or reject, but you received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.
NKJV.15: What then was the blessing you enjoyed? For I bear you witness that, if possible, you would have plucked out your own eyes and given them to me.
NKJV.16: Have I therefore become your enemy because I tell you the truth?
NKJV.17: They zealously court you, but for no good; yes, they want to exclude you, that you may be zealous for them.
NKJV.18: But it is good to be zealous in a good thing always, and not only when I am present with you.
NKJV.19: My little children, for whom I labor in birth again until Christ is formed in you,
NKJV.20: I would like to be present with you now and to change my tone; for I have doubts about you.
NKJV.21: Tell me, you who desire to be under the law, do you not hear the law?
NKJV.22: For it is written that Abraham had two sons: the one by a bondwoman, the other by a freewoman.
NKJV.23: But he who was of the bondwoman was born according to the flesh, and he of the freewoman through promise,
NKJV.24: which things are symbolic. For these are the two covenants: the one from Mount Sinai which gives birth to bondage, which is Hagar—
NKJV.25: for this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and corresponds to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children—
NKJV.26: but the Jerusalem above is free, which is the mother of us all.
NKJV.27: For it is written: “Rejoice, O barren, You who do not bear! Break forth and shout, You who are not in labor! For the desolate has many more children Than she who has a husband.”
NKJV.28: Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are children of promise.
NKJV.29: But, as he who was born according to the flesh then persecuted him who was born according to the Spirit, even so it is now.
NKJV.30: Nevertheless what does the Scripture say? “Cast out the bondwoman and her son, for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.”
NKJV.31: So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman but of the free.

ESV.1: I mean that the heir, as long as he is a child, is no different from a slave,  though he is the owner of everything,
ESV.2: but he is under guardians and managers until the date set by his father.
ESV.3: In the same way we also, when we were children, were enslaved to the elementary principles  of the world.
ESV.4: But when the fullness of time had come, God sent forth his Son, born of woman, born under the law,
ESV.5: to redeem those who were under the law, so that we might receive adoption as sons.
ESV.6: And because you are sons, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, “Abba! Father!”
ESV.7: So you are no longer a slave, but a son, and if a son, then an heir through God.
ESV.8: Formerly, when you did not know God, you were enslaved to those that by nature are not gods.
ESV.9: But now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless elementary principles of the world, whose slaves you want to be once more?
ESV.10: You observe days and months and seasons and years!
ESV.11: I am afraid I may have labored over you in vain.
ESV.12: Brothers,  I entreat you, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You did me no wrong.
ESV.13: You know it was because of a bodily ailment that I preached the gospel to you at first,
ESV.14: and though my condition was a trial to you, you did not scorn or despise me, but received me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus.
ESV.15: What then has become of your blessedness? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have gouged out your eyes and given them to me.
ESV.16: Have I then become your enemy by telling you the truth?
ESV.17: They make much of you, but for no good purpose. They want to shut you out, that you may make much of them.
ESV.18: It is always good to be made much of for a good purpose, and not only when I am present with you,
ESV.19: my little children, for whom I am again in the anguish of childbirth until Christ is formed in you!
ESV.20: I wish I could be present with you now and change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.
ESV.21: Tell me, you who desire to be under the law, do you not listen to the law?
ESV.22: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by a slave woman and one by a free woman.
ESV.23: But the son of the slave was born according to the flesh, while the son of the free woman was born through promise.
ESV.24: Now this may be interpreted allegorically: these women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children for slavery; she is Hagar.
ESV.25: Now Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia;  she corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery with her children.
ESV.26: But the Jerusalem above is free, and she is our mother.
ESV.27: For it is written, “Rejoice, O barren one who does not bear; break forth and cry aloud, you who are not in labor! For the children of the desolate one will be more than those of the one who has a husband.”
ESV.28: Now you,  brothers, like Isaac, are children of promise.
ESV.29: But just as at that time he who was born according to the flesh persecuted him who was born according to the Spirit, so also it is now.
ESV.30: But what does the Scripture say? “Cast out the slave woman and her son, for the son of the slave woman shall not inherit with the son of the free woman.”
ESV.31: So, brothers, we are not children of the slave but of the free woman.

NASB.1: Now I say, as long as the heir is a child, he does not differ at all from a slave although he is owner of everything,
NASB.2: but he is under guardians and managers until the date set by the father.
NASB.3: So also we, while we were children, were held in bondage under the elemental things of the world.
NASB.4: But when the fullness of the time came, God sent forth His Son, born of a woman, born under the Law,
NASB.5: so that He might redeem those who were under the Law, that we might receive the adoption as sons.
NASB.6: Because you are sons, God has sent forth the Spirit of His Son into our hearts, crying, 'Abba! Father!'
NASB.7: Therefore you are no longer a slave, but a son; and if a son, then an heir through God.
NASB.8: However at that time, when you did not know God, you were slaves to those which by nature are no gods.
NASB.9: But now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, how is it that you turn back again to the weak and worthless elemental things, to which you desire to be enslaved all over again?
NASB.10: You observe days and months and seasons and years.
NASB.11: I fear for you, that perhaps I have labored over you in vain.
NASB.12: I beg of you, brethren, become as I [am], for I also [have become] as you [are]. You have done me no wrong;
NASB.13: but you know that it was because of a bodily illness that I preached the gospel to you the first time;
NASB.14: and that which was a trial to you in my bodily condition you did not despise or loathe, but you received me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus [Himself].
NASB.15: Where then is that sense of blessing you had? For I bear you witness that, if possible, you would have plucked out your eyes and given them to me.
NASB.16: So have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?
NASB.17: They eagerly seek you, not commendably, but they wish to shut you out so that you will seek them.
NASB.18: But it is good always to be eagerly sought in a commendable manner, and not only when I am present with you.
NASB.19: My children, with whom I am again in labor until Christ is formed in you--
NASB.20: but I could wish to be present with you now and to change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.
NASB.21: Tell me, you who want to be under law, do you not listen to the law?
NASB.22: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the bondwoman and one by the free woman.
NASB.23: But the son by the bondwoman was born according to the flesh, and the son by the free woman through the promise.
NASB.24: This is allegorically speaking, for these [women] are two covenants: one [proceeding] from Mount Sinai bearing children who are to be slaves; she is Hagar.
NASB.25: Now this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery with her children.
NASB.26: But the Jerusalem above is free; she is our mother.
NASB.27: For it is written, 'REJOICE, BARREN WOMAN WHO DOES NOT BEAR; BREAK FORTH AND SHOUT, YOU WHO ARE NOT IN LABOR; FOR MORE NUMEROUS ARE THE CHILDREN OF THE DESOLATE THAN OF THE ONE WHO HAS A HUSBAND.'
NASB.28: And you brethren, like Isaac, are children of promise.
NASB.29: But as at that time he who was born according to the flesh persecuted him [who was born] according to the Spirit, so it is now also.
NASB.30: But what does the Scripture say? 'CAST OUT THE BONDWOMAN AND HER SON, FOR THE SON OF THE BONDWOMAN SHALL NOT BE AN HEIR WITH THE SON OF THE FREE WOMAN.'
NASB.31: So then, brethren, we are not children of a bondwoman, but of the free woman.

NLT.1: Think of it this way. If a father dies and leaves an inheritance for his young children, those children are not much better off than slaves until they grow up, even though they actually own everything their father had.
NLT.2: They have to obey their guardians until they reach whatever age their father set.
NLT.3: And that’s the way it was with us before Christ came. We were like children; we were slaves to the basic spiritual principles of this world.
NLT.4: But when the right time came, God sent his Son, born of a woman, subject to the law.
NLT.5: God sent him to buy freedom for us who were slaves to the law, so that he could adopt us as his very own children.
NLT.6: And because we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, prompting us to call out, “Abba, Father.”
NLT.7: Now you are no longer a slave but God’s own child. And since you are his child, God has made you his heir.
NLT.8: Before you Gentiles knew God, you were slaves to so-called gods that do not even exist.
NLT.9: So now that you know God (or should I say, now that God knows you), why do you want to go back again and become slaves once more to the weak and useless spiritual principles of this world?
NLT.10: You are trying to earn favor with God by observing certain days or months or seasons or years.
NLT.11: I fear for you. Perhaps all my hard work with you was for nothing.
NLT.12: Dear brothers and sisters, I plead with you to live as I do in freedom from these things, for I have become like you Gentiles — free from those laws. You did not mistreat me when I first preached to you.
NLT.13: Surely you remember that I was sick when I first brought you the Good News.
NLT.14: But even though my condition tempted you to reject me, you did not despise me or turn me away. No, you took me in and cared for me as though I were an angel from God or even Christ Jesus himself.
NLT.15: Where is that joyful and grateful spirit you felt then? I am sure you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me if it had been possible.
NLT.16: Have I now become your enemy because I am telling you the truth?
NLT.17: Those false teachers are so eager to win your favor, but their intentions are not good. They are trying to shut you off from me so that you will pay attention only to them.
NLT.18: If someone is eager to do good things for you, that’s all right; but let them do it all the time, not just when I’m with you.
NLT.19: Oh, my dear children! I feel as if I’m going through labor pains for you again, and they will continue until Christ is fully developed in your lives.
NLT.20: I wish I were with you right now so I could change my tone. But at this distance I don’t know how else to help you.
NLT.21: Tell me, you who want to live under the law, do you know what the law actually says?
NLT.22: The Scriptures say that Abraham had two sons, one from his slave wife and one from his freeborn wife.
NLT.23: The son of the slave wife was born in a human attempt to bring about the fulfillment of God’s promise. But the son of the freeborn wife was born as God’s own fulfillment of his promise.
NLT.24: These two women serve as an illustration of God’s two covenants. The first woman, Hagar, represents Mount Sinai where people received the law that enslaved them.
NLT.25: And now Jerusalem is just like Mount Sinai in Arabia, because she and her children live in slavery to the law.
NLT.26: But the other woman, Sarah, represents the heavenly Jerusalem. She is the free woman, and she is our mother.
NLT.27:
As Isaiah said, “Rejoice, O childless woman,
you who have never given birth!
Break into a joyful shout,
you who have never been in labor!
For the desolate woman now has more children
than the woman who lives with her husband!”
NLT.28: And you, dear brothers and sisters, are children of the promise, just like Isaac.
NLT.29: But you are now being persecuted by those who want you to keep the law, just as Ishmael, the child born by human effort, persecuted Isaac, the child born by the power of the Spirit.
NLT.30: But what do the Scriptures say about that? “Get rid of the slave and her son, for the son of the slave woman will not share the inheritance with the free woman’s son.”
NLT.31: So, dear brothers and sisters, we are not children of the slave woman; we are children of the free woman.

WEB.1: But I say that so long as the heir is a child, he is no different from a bondservant, though he is lord of all;
WEB.2: but is under guardians and stewards until the day appointed by the father.
WEB.3: So we also, when we were children, were held in bondage under the elemental principles of the world.
WEB.4: But when the fullness of the time came, God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law,
WEB.5: that he might redeem those who were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of children.
WEB.6: And because you are children, God sent out the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, “Abba,  Father!”
WEB.7: So you are no longer a bondservant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
WEB.8: However at that time, not knowing God, you were in bondage to those who by nature are not gods.
WEB.9: But now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, why do you turn back again to the weak and miserable elemental principles, to which you desire to be in bondage all over again?
WEB.10: You observe days, months, seasons, and years.
WEB.11: I am afraid for you, that I might have wasted my labor for you.
WEB.12: I beg you, brothers, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You did me no wrong,
WEB.13: but you know that because of weakness of the flesh I preached the Good News to you the first time.
WEB.14: That which was a temptation to you in my flesh, you didn’t despise nor reject; but you received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.
WEB.15: What was the blessing you enjoyed? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have plucked out your eyes and given them to me.
WEB.16: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?
WEB.17: They zealously seek you in no good way. No, they desire to alienate you, that you may seek them.
WEB.18: But it is always good to be zealous in a good cause, and not only when I am present with you.
WEB.19: My little children, of whom I am again in travail until Christ is formed in you —
WEB.20: but I could wish to be present with you now, and to change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.
WEB.21: Tell me, you that desire to be under the law, don’t you listen to the law?
WEB.22: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the servant, and one by the free woman.
WEB.23: However, the son by the servant was born according to the flesh, but the son by the free woman was born through promise.
WEB.24: These things contain an allegory, for these are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children to bondage, which is Hagar.
WEB.25: For this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and answers to the Jerusalem that exists now, for she is in bondage with her children.
WEB.26: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, which is the mother of us all.
WEB.27: For it is written, “Rejoice, you barren who don’t bear. Break out and shout, you that don’t travail. For more are the children of the desolate than of her who has a husband.”
WEB.28: Now we, brothers, as Isaac was, are children of promise.
WEB.29: But as then, he who was born according to the flesh persecuted him who was born according to the Spirit, so also it is now.
WEB.30: However what does the Scripture say? “Throw out the servant and her son, for the son of the servant will not inherit with the son of the free woman.”
WEB.31: So then, brothers, we are not children of a servant, but of the free woman.

CSB17.1: Now I say that as long as the heir <sup>ⓔ</sup> is a child, he differs in no way from a slave, though he is the owner <sup>ⓕ</sup> of everything.
CSB17.2: Instead, he is under guardians and trustees until the time set by his father.
CSB17.3: In the same way we also, when we were children, were in slavery under the elements <sup>[1]</sup> of the world. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.4: When the time came to completion, God sent his Son, <sup>ⓗ</sup> born of a woman, <sup>ⓘ</sup> born under the law,
CSB17.5: to redeem those under the law, so that we might receive adoption as sons. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.6: And because you are sons, God sent the Spirit of his Son ⓚ into our [2] hearts, crying, “Abba, [3] Father!” ⓛ
CSB17.7: So you are no longer a slave <sup>ⓜ</sup> but a son, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and if a son, then God has made you an heir. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.8: But in the past, since you didn’t know God, <sup>ⓑ</sup> you were enslaved to things <sup>[4]</sup> that by nature are not gods.
CSB17.9: But now, since you know God, <sup>ⓒ</sup> or rather have become known by God, how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless <sup>ⓓ</sup> elements? <sup>ⓔ</sup> Do you want to be enslaved to them all over again?
CSB17.10: You are observing special days, months, seasons, and years. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.11: I am fearful for you, that perhaps my labor for you has been wasted.
CSB17.12: I beg you, brothers and sisters: Become like me, for I also became like you. You have not wronged me;
CSB17.13: you know <sup>[5]</sup> that previously I preached the gospel to you because of a weakness of the flesh.
CSB17.14: You did not despise or reject me though my physical condition was a trial for you. <sup>[6]</sup> On the contrary, you received me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus himself.
CSB17.15: Where, then, is your blessing? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have torn out your eyes and given them to me.
CSB17.16: So then, have I become your enemy because I told you the truth? <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.17: They court you eagerly, but not for good. They want to exclude you from me, so that you would pursue them.
CSB17.18: But it is always good to be pursued <sup>[7]</sup> in a good manner — and not just when I am with you.
CSB17.19: My children, <sup>ⓗ</sup> I am again suffering labor pains <sup>ⓘ</sup> for you until Christ is formed in you.
CSB17.20: I would like to be with you right now and change my tone of voice, because I don’t know what to do about you.
CSB17.21: Tell me, you who want to be under the law, don’t you hear the law?
CSB17.22: For it is written that Abraham <sup>ⓙ</sup> had two sons, one by a slave <sup>ⓚ</sup> and the other by a free woman.
CSB17.23: But the one by the slave was born as a result of the flesh, while the one by the free woman was born through promise.
CSB17.24: These things are being taken figuratively, for the women represent two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai <sup>ⓛ</sup> and bears children into slavery — this is Hagar. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.25: Now Hagar represents Mount Sinai in Arabia <sup>ⓝ</sup> and corresponds to the present Jerusalem, <sup>ⓐ</sup> for she is in slavery with her children.
CSB17.26: But the Jerusalem above <sup>ⓑ</sup> is free, and she is our mother.
CSB17.27:
For it is written, ⓒ Rejoice, childless woman, ⓓ
unable to give birth.
Burst into song and shout,
you who are not in labor,
for the children of the desolate woman will be many,
more numerous than those
of the woman who has a husband. [8] ⓔ
CSB17.28: Now you too, brothers and sisters, like Isaac, <sup>ⓕ</sup> are children of promise. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.29: But just as then the child born as a result of the flesh persecuted the one born as a result of the Spirit, <sup>ⓗ</sup> so also now. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.30: But what does the Scripture say? “Drive out the slave and her son, for the son of the slave will never be a coheir with the son of the free woman.” [9] ⓙ
CSB17.31: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we are not children of a slave but of the free woman. <sup>ⓚ</sup>

AMP.1: Now what I mean [when I talk about children and their guardians] is this: as long as the heir is a child, he does not differ at all from a slave even though he is the [future owner and] master of all [the estate];
AMP.2: but he is under [the authority of] guardians and household administrators or managers until the date set by his father [when he is of legal age].
AMP.3: So also we [whether Jews or Gentiles], when we were children (spiritually immature), were kept like slaves under the elementary [man-made religious or philosophical] teachings of the world.
AMP.4: But when [in God's plan] the proper time had fully come, God sent His Son, born of a woman, born under the [regulations of the] Law,
AMP.5: so that He might redeem and liberate those who were under the Law, that we [who believe] might be adopted as sons [as God's children with all rights as fully grown members of a family].
AMP.6: And because you [really] are [His] sons, God has sent the Spirit of His Son into our hearts, crying out,"Abba! Father!"
AMP.7: Therefore you are no longer a slave (bond-servant), but a son; and if a son, then also an heir through [the gracious act of] God [through Christ].
AMP.8: But at that time, when you did not know [the true] God and were unacquainted with Him, you [Gentiles] were slaves to those [pagan] things which by [their very] nature were not and could not be gods at all.
AMP.9: Now, however, since you have come to know [the true] God [through personal experience], or rather to be known by God, how is it that you are turning back again to the weak and worthless elemental principles [of religions and philosophies], to which you want to be enslaved all over again?
AMP.10: [For example,] you observe [particular] days and months and seasons and years.
AMP.11: I fear for you, that perhaps I have labored [to the point of exhaustion] over you in vain.
AMP.12: Believers, I beg of you, become as I am [free from the bondage of Jewish ritualism and ordinances], for I have become as you are [a Gentile]. You did me no wrong [when I first came to you; do not do it now].
AMP.13: On the contrary, you know that it was because of a physical illness that I [remained and] preached the gospel to you the first time;
AMP.14: and even though my physical condition was a trial to you, you did not regard it with contempt, or scorn and reject me; but you received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus Himself.
AMP.15: What then has become of that sense of blessing and the joy that you once had [from your salvation and your relationship with Christ]? For I testify of you that, if possible, you would have torn out your own eyes and given them to me [ to replace mine].
AMP.16: So have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?
AMP.17: These men [the Judaizers] eagerly seek you [to entrap you with honeyed words and attention, to win you over to their philosophy], not honorably [for their purpose is not honorable or worthy of consideration]. They want to isolate you [from us who oppose them] so that you will seek them.
AMP.18: Now it is always pleasant to be eagerly sought after [provided that it is] for a good purpose, and not just when I am with you [seeking you myself—but beware of the others doing it].
AMP.19: My little children, for whom I am again in [the pains of] labor until Christ is [completely and permanently] formed within you—
AMP.20: how I wish that I were with you now and could change my tone, because I am perplexed in regard to you.
AMP.21: Tell me, you who are bent on being under the Law, do you not listen to [what] the Law [really says]?
AMP.22: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman [Hagar] and one by the free woman [Sarah].
AMP.23: But the child of the slave woman was born according to the flesh and had an ordinary birth, while the son of the free woman was born in fulfillment of the promise.
AMP.24: Now these facts are about to be used [by me] as an allegory [that is, I will illustrate by using them]: for these women can represent two covenants: one [covenant originated] from Mount Sinai [where the Law was given] that bears children [destined] for slavery; she is Hagar.
AMP.25: Now Hagar is (represents) Mount Sinai in Arabia and she corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery with her children.
AMP.26: But the Jerusalem above [that is, the way of faith, represented by Sarah] is free; she is our mother.
AMP.27:
For it is written [in the Scriptures],
"Rejoice, o barren woman who has not given birth;
Break forth into a [joyful] shout, you who are not in labor;
For the desolate woman has many more children
Than she who has a husband."
AMP.28: And we, [believing] brothers and sisters, like Isaac, are children [not merely of physical descent, like Ishmael, but are children born] of promise [born miraculously].
AMP.29: But as at that time the child [of ordinary birth] born according to the flesh persecuted the son who was born according to [the promise and working of] the Spirit, so it is now also.
AMP.30:
But what does the Scripture say?
"Cast out the bondwoman [Hagar] and her son [Ishmael],
For never shall the son of the bondwoman be heir and share the inheritance with the son of the free woman."
AMP.31: So then, believers, we [who are born again—reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, and set apart for His purpose] are not children of a slave woman [the natural], but of the free woman [the supernatural].

MSG.1: Let me show you the implications of this. As long as the heir is a minor, he has no advantage over the slave. Though legally he owns the entire inheritance,
MSG.2: he is subject to tutors and administrators until whatever date the father has set for emancipation.
MSG.3: That is the way it is with us: When we were minors, we were just like slaves ordered around by simple instructions (the tutors and administrators of this world), with no say in the conduct of our own lives.
MSG.4: But when the time arrived that was set by God the Father, God sent his Son, born among us of a woman, born under the conditions of the law so that he might redeem those of us who have been kidnapped by the law.
MSG.5: Thus we have been set free to experience our rightful heritage.
MSG.6: You can tell for sure that you are now fully adopted as his own children because God sent the Spirit of his Son into our lives crying out, "Papa! Father!"
MSG.7: Doesn't that privilege of intimate conversation with God make it plain that you are not a slave, but a child? And if you are a child, you're also an heir, with complete access to the inheritance.
MSG.8: Earlier, before you knew God personally, you were enslaved to so-called gods that had nothing of the divine about them.
MSG.9: But now that you know the real God—or rather since God knows you—how can you possibly subject yourselves again to those paper tigers?
MSG.10: For that is exactly what you do when you are intimidated into scrupulously observing all the traditions, taboos, and superstitions associated with special days and seasons and years.
MSG.11: I am afraid that all my hard work among you has gone up in a puff of smoke!
MSG.12: My dear friends, what I would really like you to do is try to put yourselves in my shoes to the same extent that I, when I was with you, put myself in yours. You were very sensitive and kind then. You did not come down on me personally.
MSG.13: You were well aware that the reason I ended up preaching to you was that I was physically broken, and so, prevented from continuing my journey, I was forced to stop with you. That is how I came to preach to you.
MSG.14: And don't you remember that even though taking in a sick guest was most troublesome for you, you chose to treat me as well as you would have treated an angel of God—as well as you would have treated Jesus himself if he had visited you?
MSG.15: What has happened to the satisfaction you felt at that time? There were some of you then who, if possible, would have given your very eyes to me—that is how deeply you cared!
MSG.16: And now have I suddenly become your enemy simply by telling you the truth? I can't believe it.
MSG.17: Those heretical teachers go to great lengths to flatter you, but their motives are rotten. They want to shut you out of the free world of God's grace so that you will always depend on them for approval and direction, making them feel important.
MSG.18: It is a good thing to be ardent in doing good, but not just when I am in your presence. Can't you continue the same concern for both my person and my message when I am away from you that you had when I was with you?
MSG.19: Do you know how I feel right now, and will feel until Christ's life becomes visible in your lives? Like a mother in the pain of childbirth.
MSG.20: Oh, I keep wishing that I was with you. Then I wouldn't be reduced to this blunt, letter-writing language out of sheer frustration.
MSG.21: Tell me now, you who have become so enamored with the law: Have you paid close attention to that law?
MSG.22: Abraham, remember, had two sons: one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
MSG.23: The son of the slave woman was born by human connivance; the son of the free woman was born by God's promise.
MSG.24: This illustrates the very thing we are dealing with now. The two births represent two ways of being in relationship with God. One is from Mount Sinai in Arabia.
MSG.25: It corresponds with what is now going on in Jerusalem—a slave life, producing slaves as offspring. This is the way of Hagar.
MSG.26: In contrast to that, there is an invisible Jerusalem, a free Jerusalem, and she is our mother—this is the way of Sarah.
MSG.27: Remember what Isaiah wrote: Rejoice, barren woman who bears no children, shout and cry out, woman who has no birth pangs, Because the children of the barren woman now surpass the children of the chosen woman.
MSG.28: Isn't it clear, friends, that you, like Isaac, are children of promise?
MSG.29: In the days of Hagar and Sarah, the child who came from faithless connivance (Ishmael) harassed the child who came—empowered by the Spirit—from the faithful promise (Isaac). Isn't it clear that the harassment you are now experiencing from the Jerusalem heretics follows that old pattern?
MSG.30: There is a Scripture that tells us what to do: "Expel the slave mother with her son, for the slave son will not inherit with the free son."
MSG.31: Isn't that conclusive? We are not children of the slave woman, but of the free woman.

NIV.1: What I am saying is that as long as the heir is a child, he is no different from a slave, although he owns the whole estate.
NIV.2: He is subject to guardians and trustees until the time set by his father.
NIV.3: So also, when we were children, we were in slavery under the basic principles of the world.
NIV.4: But when the time had fully come, God sent his Son, born of a woman, born under law,
NIV.5: to redeem those under law, that we might receive the full rights of sons.
NIV.6: Because you are sons, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, the Spirit who calls out, “Abba, Father.”
NIV.7: So you are no longer a slave, but a son; and since you are a son, God has made you also an heir.
NIV.8:
Paul’s Concern for the Galatians
Formerly, when you did not know God, you were slaves to those who by nature are not gods.
NIV.9: But now that you know God—or rather are known by God—how is it that you are turning back to those weak and miserable principles? Do you wish to be enslaved by them all over again?
NIV.10: You are observing special days and months and seasons and years!
NIV.11: I fear for you, that somehow I have wasted my efforts on you.
NIV.12: I plead with you, brothers, become like me, for I became like you. You have done me no wrong.
NIV.13: As you know, it was because of an illness that I first preached the gospel to you.
NIV.14: Even though my illness was a trial to you, you did not treat me with contempt or scorn. Instead, you welcomed me as if I were an angel of God, as if I were Christ Jesus himself.
NIV.15: What has happened to all your joy? I can testify that, if you could have done so, you would have torn out your eyes and given them to me.
NIV.16: Have I now become your enemy by telling you the truth?
NIV.17: Those people are zealous to win you over, but for no good. What they want is to alienate you from us, so that you may be zealous for them.
NIV.18: It is fine to be zealous, provided the purpose is good, and to be so always and not just when I am with you.
NIV.19: My dear children, for whom I am again in the pains of childbirth until Christ is formed in you,
NIV.20: how I wish I could be with you now and change my tone, because I am perplexed about you!
NIV.21:
Hagar and Sarah
Tell me, you who want to be under the law, are you not aware of what the law says?
NIV.22: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and the other by the free woman.
NIV.23: His son by the slave woman was born in the ordinary way; but his son by the free woman was born as the result of a promise.
NIV.24: These things may be taken figuratively, for the women represent two covenants. One covenant is from Mount Sinai and bears children who are to be slaves: This is Hagar.
NIV.25: Now Hagar stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present city of Jerusalem, because she is in slavery with her children.
NIV.26: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
NIV.27:
For it is written:
“Be glad, O barren woman,
who bears no children;
break forth and cry aloud,
you who have no labor pains;
because more are the children of the desolate woman
than of her who has a husband.”
NIV.28: Now you, brothers, like Isaac, are children of promise.
NIV.29: At that time the son born in the ordinary way persecuted the son born by the power of the Spirit. It is the same now.
NIV.30: But what does the Scripture say? “Get rid of the slave woman and her son, for the slave woman’s son will never share in the inheritance with the free woman’s son.”
NIV.31: Therefore, brothers, we are not children of the slave woman, but of the free woman.

NRSVCE.1: My point is this: heirs, as long as they are minors, are no better than slaves, though they are the owners of all the property;
NRSVCE.2: but they remain under guardians and trustees until the date set by the father.
NRSVCE.3: So with us; while we were minors, we were enslaved to the elemental spirits of the world.
NRSVCE.4: But when the fullness of time had come, God sent his Son, born of a woman, born under the law,
NRSVCE.5: in order to redeem those who were under the law, so that we might receive adoption as children.
NRSVCE.6: And because you are children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, “Abba! Father!”
NRSVCE.7: So you are no longer a slave but a child, and if a child then also an heir, through God.
NRSVCE.8: Formerly, when you did not know God, you were enslaved to beings that by nature are not gods.
NRSVCE.9: Now, however, that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, how can you turn back again to the weak and beggarly elemental spirits? How can you want to be enslaved to them again?
NRSVCE.10: You are observing special days, and months, and seasons, and years.
NRSVCE.11: I am afraid that my work for you may have been wasted.
NRSVCE.12: Friends, I beg you, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You have done me no wrong.
NRSVCE.13: You know that it was because of a physical infirmity that I first announced the gospel to you;
NRSVCE.14: though my condition put you to the test, you did not scorn or despise me, but welcomed me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.15: What has become of the goodwill you felt? For I testify that, had it been possible, you would have torn out your eyes and given them to me.
NRSVCE.16: Have I now become your enemy by telling you the truth?
NRSVCE.17: They make much of you, but for no good purpose; they want to exclude you, so that you may make much of them.
NRSVCE.18: It is good to be made much of for a good purpose at all times, and not only when I am present with you.
NRSVCE.19: My little children, for whom I am again in the pain of childbirth until Christ is formed in you,
NRSVCE.20: I wish I were present with you now and could change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.
NRSVCE.21: Tell me, you who desire to be subject to the law, will you not listen to the law?
NRSVCE.22: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by a slave woman and the other by a free woman.
NRSVCE.23: One, the child of the slave, was born according to the flesh; the other, the child of the free woman, was born through the promise.
NRSVCE.24: Now this is an allegory: these women are two covenants. One woman, in fact, is Hagar, from Mount Sinai, bearing children for slavery.
NRSVCE.25: Now Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery with her children.
NRSVCE.26: But the other woman corresponds to the Jerusalem above; she is free, and she is our mother.
NRSVCE.27:
For it is written, “Rejoice, you childless one, you who bear no children,
burst into song and shout, you who endure no birth pangs;
for the children of the desolate woman are more numerous
than the children of the one who is married.”
NRSVCE.28: Now you, my friends, are children of the promise, like Isaac.
NRSVCE.29: But just as at that time the child who was born according to the flesh persecuted the child who was born according to the Spirit, so it is now also.
NRSVCE.30: But what does the scripture say? “Drive out the slave and her child; for the child of the slave will not share the inheritance with the child of the free woman.”
NRSVCE.31: So then, friends, we are children, not of the slave but of the free woman.

NET.1: Now I mean that the heir, as long as he is a minor, is no different from a slave, though he is the owner of everything.
NET.2: But he is under guardians and managers until the date set by his father.
NET.3: So also we, when we were minors, were enslaved under the basic forces of the world.
NET.4: But when the appropriate time had come, God sent out his Son, born of a woman, born under the law,
NET.5: to redeem those who were under the law, so that we may be adopted as sons with full rights.
NET.6: And because you are sons, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, who calls “Abba! Father!”
NET.7: So you are no longer a slave but a son, and if you are a son, then you are also an heir through God.
NET.8: Formerly when you did not know God, you were enslaved to beings that by nature are not gods at all.
NET.9: But now that you have come to know God (or rather to be known by God), how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless basic forces? Do you want to be enslaved to them all over again?
NET.10: You are observing religious days and months and seasons and years.
NET.11: I fear for you that my work for you may have been in vain.
NET.12: I beg you, brothers and sisters, become like me, because I have become like you. You have done me no wrong!
NET.13: But you know it was because of a physical illness that I first proclaimed the gospel to you,
NET.14: and though my physical condition put you to the test, you did not despise or reject me. Instead, you welcomed me as though I were an angel of God, as though I were Christ Jesus himself!
NET.15: Where then is your sense of happiness now? For I testify about you that if it were possible, you would have pulled out your eyes and given them to me!
NET.16: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?
NET.17: They court you eagerly, but for no good purpose; they want to exclude you, so that you would seek them eagerly.
NET.18: However, it is good to be sought eagerly for a good purpose at all times, and not only when I am present with you.
NET.19: My children — I am again undergoing birth pains until Christ is formed in you!
NET.20: I wish I could be with you now and change my tone of voice, because I am perplexed about you.
NET.21: Tell me, you who want to be under the law, do you not understand the law?
NET.22: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and the other by the free woman.
NET.23: But one, the son by the slave woman, was born by natural descent, while the other, the son by the free woman, was born through the promise.
NET.24: These things may be treated as an allegory, for these women represent two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai bearing children for slavery; this is Hagar.
NET.25: Now Hagar represents Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery with her children.
NET.26: But the Jerusalem above is free, and she is our mother.
NET.27: For it is written: “Rejoice, O barren woman who does not bear children; break forth and shout, you who have no birth pains, because the children of the desolate woman are more numerous than those of the woman who has a husband.”
NET.28: But you, brothers and sisters, are children of the promise like Isaac.
NET.29: But just as at that time the one born by natural descent persecuted the one born according to the Spirit, so it is now.
NET.30: But what does the scripture say? “Throw out the slave woman and her son, for the son of the slave woman will not share the inheritance with the son” of the free woman.
NET.31: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we are not children of the slave woman but of the free woman.
